title: tricoryne elatior var. scabra
intro: Tricoryne elatior var. scabra, commonly known as the scabrous tricoryne or prairie onion, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It is native to Australia, where it is often found in dry, sandy soils. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities, which can help to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The scabrous tricoryne can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including coughs, colds, and sore throats.
